expected_trades_per_day_basis: Synthesizer notes ~85 triggers in the ~30-day backtest window (85/30≈2.8); tight filter (OI -8% AND taker_buy>=0.60 AND high-liquidity) keeps frequency low vs E126's ~115/day.

best_in: High-funding overheated shorts being squeezed (negative funding swing within 1h)
BTC-led recovery sessions where alts rebound after a coordinated OI flush
Asia/EU session opens where OI unwinds resolve into short-covers within 5-15m
worst_in: Strong sustained downtrends where OI -8% is just continuation selling (not capitulation)
Low-volatility chop where taker_buy_ratio>=0.60 is noise, not real bid
BTC dump regimes — alt rebounds get cut short by index drag
regime_sensitivity.explanation: This is a counter-trend reversal entry on the OI_Price channel, which the registry shows is biased short — going long here only pays when the OI crash IS the capitulation, not the start. Taker buy dominance during OI reduction is the short-squeeze signature, so it works best when shorts are already crowded and fails when sellers still have ammo.

live_readiness_caveats: Counter-trend geometry: a single missed SL in a true continuation crash will erase 5-10 winners — execution latency >3s on entry will materially worsen fills since reversals snap fast

High-liquidity bucket filter helps slippage but concentrates trades in BTC/ETH/SOL/major alts where OI -8% is rarer and often news-driven — expect bursty distribution (0 trades for 2 days, then 4 in one hour)
OI data freshness matters: if the OI feed lags >30s the -8% threshold will fire AFTER the squeeze has already played out, killing edge

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: drop >15pp below backtest win-rate within first 20 trades
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Reversal entries are fragile — win-rate is the first thing that breaks when the regime no longer supports short-squeezes

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: if median time-to-SL < median time-to-TP, kill
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Healthy reversals resolve fast on the winning side; if losses come faster than wins, you're catching falling knives not squeezes
Counter-trend strategies cluster losses in trending regimes; 4+ streak is the canary that the macro regime flipped against the setup
OI crash + taker buy = thin book moment; slippage directly eats the tight TP this archetype needs (forced SL<=1.5x TP per synthesizer)
Far below predicts filter is too tight in current regime (no signal); far above means OI threshold is firing on a regime shift (overfit risk)

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling expectancy turns negative AND BTC 7-day return < -5%
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Confirms the long-reversal thesis is broken in a sustained bear regime — this is exactly the regime an OI_Price short-biased channel exists for

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 3 consecutive trades hit full SL with zero TP touch
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Indicates OI -8% is signaling continuation not capitulation; the short-squeeze geometry has inverted and the edge is gone

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Live mean trade % stays below 50% of backtest mean for 30+ trades
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: E126 paper-shadow lesson: backtest score can compound to negative on small accounts — sustained underperformance vs backtest = the asymmetric tail (left side) is realizing live and the legacy metric was hiding it

expected_max_dd_basis: With ~2.8 trades/day × 30 days × 5-10% sizing and synthesizer-forced SL<=1.5x TP, a 4-loss streak (plausible on counter-trend) at 1.5x SL = ~6% account DD, padded to 7.5% for slippage and clustering during BTC drawdown days.
compare_to_existing: E066 pc_crash_taker_buy_long (best=+0.339, trig=3684) — same crash+taker-buy reversal logic but on the pricechange channel with no OI gate; E204 should behave like a tighter, lower-frequency, higher-conviction sibling. Triangulate: if E066 is alive in paper-shadow and E204 underperforms it on per-trade mean, the OI gate is not adding edge and E204 should be retired.
